contentBlocks.title: Why convert PNG to AVIF?
contentBlocks.html:
Portable Network Graphics (PNG) is a lossless image format supporting millions colors. It is widely used for graphics with transparency and is supported by virtually every browser and device.

Converting PNG to AVIF (AV1 Image File Format) significantly reduces file size, making images faster to load and easier to share.

AVIF supports alpha transparency, preserving any transparent areas in your image. AVIF supports both lossy and lossless compression, giving you control over the quality-to-size ratio.

This converter processes everything locally in your browser – your files never leave your device. No uploads, no servers, no registration. Convert as many PNG files as you need, completely free.

items.description: Choose your preferred quality and output options. The converter shows a live preview so you can compare the original PNG with the AVIF result before downloading.

items.title: Download your AVIF file
items.description: Click the download button to save your converted AVIF file. For multiple files, use the batch download option to get all results in one click.

contentBlocks.title: PNG to AVIF conversion in practice
contentBlocks.html:
Businesses, developers, and creators worldwide rely on image format compatibility. Whether you are preparing product images for Amazon, eBay, Etsy, Shopify, optimizing graphics for WordPress, Wix, Squarespace, or converting photos for government portals worldwide, having the right format matters.

Modern web standards increasingly favor efficient formats. Google PageSpeed Insights and Core Web Vitals metrics reward pages that use optimized images. Converting PNG to AVIF can directly improve your Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) score, which impacts both search rankings and user experience.

For email marketing campaigns and social media, AVIF provides excellent compression while maintaining visual quality. Whether you are a freelancer, small business owner, or enterprise user, this converter handles your image conversion needs reliably and securely.

contentBlocks.title: Tips for converting PNG to AVIF
contentBlocks.html:
• Start with quality 85 – this is typically the sweet spot for AVIF compression, offering significant file size reduction with minimal visible quality loss.
  • Batch convert for efficiency – if you have many PNG files, use the batch conversion feature to process them all at once. This saves considerable time compared to one-by-one conversion.
  • Compare before downloading – use the live preview to compare the original PNG with the AVIF result. Adjust quality settings until you find the right balance for your specific use case.
  • Keep your originals – always save a copy of your original PNG files. Lossy compression is irreversible, so having the originals allows you to re-convert with different settings later.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about PNG to AVIF conversion

items.question: Is converting PNG to AVIF free?
items.answer: Yes, this PNG to AVIF converter is completely free with no limits. You can convert as many files as you need without registration or watermarks.

items.question: Does the converter upload my files to a server?
items.answer: No. All processing happens locally in your browser using JavaScript and Canvas API. Your files never leave your device, ensuring complete privacy and security.

items.question: Will I lose quality when converting PNG to AVIF?
items.answer: AVIF supports lossless compression, so you can preserve the original image quality completely. If AVIF also supports lossy mode, you can choose lower quality for smaller files.

items.question: Can I convert multiple PNG files at once?
items.answer: Yes, the converter supports batch conversion. You can add multiple PNG files and convert them all to AVIF simultaneously. Use the batch download option to save all converted files at once.

items.question: Do all browsers support AVIF?
items.answer: AVIF is supported by Chrome 85+, Firefox 93+, Safari 16.4+. For older browsers that don't support AVIF, you can use the HTML <picture> element to provide a fallback format like JPG or PNG.

items.question: What is the maximum file size I can convert?
items.answer: There is no hard file size limit since processing happens in your browser. However, very large files (over 50 MB) may take longer to process depending on your device's available memory and processing power.

items.question: Does the converter work on mobile devices?
items.answer: Yes, the converter works on any device with a modern web browser, including smartphones and tablets. The interface adapts to your screen size for a comfortable experience on any device.
